quiz.title: MSSQL Query Writing Concepts
quiz.subtitle: Understanding SQL Server Query Writing
quiz.description: Test your conceptual understanding of how query writing works in Microsoft SQL Server. Focus is on system behavior, query logic, and design, not memorizing keywords.

questions.text: What is the main purpose of a SELECT statement in SQL Server?

options.text: To retrieve data from one or more tables
To delete data from a table

questions.explanation: SELECT is used to retrieve data from tables.

questions.text: Which clause is used to filter rows returned by a query?
questions.explanation: WHERE filters rows before grouping or ordering.

questions.text: What does the GROUP BY clause do in a query?

options.text: Aggregates rows based on column values

questions.explanation: GROUP BY is used for aggregation.

questions.text: Why is it important to use indexes in SQL Server?
questions.explanation: Indexes speed up data retrieval.

questions.text: What is the result of a JOIN operation?

options.text: Combines rows from two or more tables based on a related column

questions.explanation: JOIN combines data from tables.

questions.text: What does the DISTINCT keyword do in a query?

options.text: Removes duplicate rows from the result set

questions.explanation: DISTINCT removes duplicates.

questions.text: Why should you use parameterized queries?

options.text: To store passwords in plain text

questions.explanation: Parameterized queries help prevent SQL injection.

wrongAnswerFeedback.B: They improve security, not slow down queries.
wrongAnswerFeedback.D: Never store passwords in plain text.

questions.text: How does SQL Server process a query with both WHERE and HAVING clauses?

options.text: WHERE filters rows before grouping, HAVING filters after grouping
HAVING filters before grouping, WHERE after

questions.explanation: WHERE filters rows before grouping, HAVING filters groups after aggregation.

questions.text: What is a subquery and how is it used in SQL Server?

options.text: A query nested inside another query, used to return data for the outer query

questions.explanation: A subquery is a query within another query, often in WHERE or FROM clauses.

questions.text: Why might a query with a function in the WHERE clause run slowly?

options.text: Functions can prevent index usage, causing full table scans

questions.explanation: Functions in WHERE can prevent indexes from being used, slowing queries.

questions.text: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN?

options.text: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows; LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching from the right
LEFT JOIN returns only matching rows
INNER JOIN returns all rows from both tables

questions.explanation: INNER JOIN returns only matches; LEFT JOIN includes all left table rows.

wrongAnswerFeedback.B: LEFT JOIN includes all left rows.

questions.text: How does SQL Server handle NULL values in comparisons?

options.text: NULL compared to any value (even NULL) is unknown

questions.explanation: NULL is not equal to anything, not even another NULL.

wrongAnswerFeedback.D: NULL is not equal to any value.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the ORDER BY clause?
questions.explanation: ORDER BY sorts the results.

questions.text: How does SQL Server optimize query execution?

options.text: It creates an execution plan based on statistics and indexes
It runs queries in the order written
It always scans the whole table

questions.explanation: SQL Server uses execution plans for optimization.

wrongAnswerFeedback.B: Order of writing does not affect execution order.

questions.text: What is a CTE (Common Table Expression) and why use it?

options.text: A temporary result set used for organizing complex queries

questions.explanation: CTEs help structure complex queries and improve readability.

questions.text: Why is it important to understand transaction isolation levels?

options.text: They control how and when changes made by one operation become visible to others

questions.explanation: Isolation levels affect concurrency and consistency.

questions.text: How does SQL Server handle deadlocks?

options.text: It detects deadlocks and chooses a victim to rollback

questions.explanation: SQL Server resolves deadlocks by rolling back one transaction.

questions.text: What is the purpose of using aliases in queries?

options.text: To give tables or columns temporary names for readability

questions.explanation: Aliases improve query readability.

questions.text: How does the TOP clause affect query results?

options.text: It limits the number of rows returned

questions.explanation: TOP limits the number of rows returned.

questions.text: Why should you avoid using SELECT * in production queries?

options.text: It can return unnecessary columns, reduce performance, and break code if schema changes

questions.explanation: SELECT * can cause performance and maintenance issues.
